introSection:
Roller Coaster was a steel roller coaster which operated at Dorney Park in Pennsylvania, USA and subsequently at Coney Park in Jamaica.

The ride opened at Dorney Park in 1977 as Flying Dutchman. After operating at Dorney Park, it was sold to Coney Park in Jamaica which opened in 1989. The name "Roller Coaster" is assumed, the actual name is not known at this time.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride had red track and white supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The cars were designed to mimic the Ford Mustang of the late 60's.

sections.title: Images
sections.content: Pictures of the ride at Dorney Park.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Pictures of a model of the roller coaster

fullText:
Roller Coaster was a steel roller coaster which operated at Dorney Park in Pennsylvania, USA and subsequently at Coney Park in Jamaica.

The ride opened at Dorney Park in 1977 as Flying Dutchman. After operating at Dorney Park, it was sold to Coney Park in Jamaica which opened in 1989. The name "Roller Coaster" is assumed, the actual name is not known at this time.

The ride had red track and white supports.

The cars were designed to mimic the Ford Mustang of the late 60's.

Pictures of the ride at Dorney Park.


 * Pictures of a model of the roller coaster
